content:
Hello everyone, upstream rust now has merged the UnsafePinned type (still unstable of-course).

In my rust: miscdevice: Add additional data to MiscDeviceRegistration patchset I've manually implemented this type before it was in Upsteam.

For the next version I want to match the API 1to1 to the Upstream rust version and possibly use the same documentation, so that once the type is stable we can just alias kernel::type::UnsafePinned to core::ptr::UnsafePinned.

Is copying the documentation (and probably parts of the code) alright License wise and how would attribution be handled in such a case?

Is copying the documentation (and probably parts of the code) alright License wise and how would attribution be handled in such a case?

It should be fine as long as the license/copyright is respected. Please take a look at how we did it in the std_vendor.rs files (and the commits introducing those).

Another source of inspiration may be alloc, that was an entire library we added from upstream that is now gone, see commit 753dece88d70 ("rust: import upstream alloc crate").
I also asked on the Rust PR: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/137043#issuecomment-2815348311

@Boqun Feng this is very interesting for the pcpu work, since it allows "This means, in particular, that &mut UnsafePinned is not necessarily a unique pointer, and thus the compiler cannot just make aliasing assumptions. "
Yeah, but it's not a free ticket, if you provide a public API, you will have to provide a Pin<&mut UnsafePinned>, because &mut UnsafePinned alone is not immune to swap().

One of the usage of UnsafePinned is that you already some T : Sync (i.e. having interior mutability), but for a few special functions you want to provide exclusive accesses (only one thread can do it at a time), then you can provide a function foo(Pin<&mut T>) where T is a UnsafePinned. This can make sure 1) no concurrent foo() called while foo() being called* and 2) compiler won't assume nolias when foo() being called, so a co-existing &T is not a UB.

*) The API has to guarantee no co-existing Pin<&mut T>.
Sorry, I'm not sure I'm understanding what this has to do with mutual exclusion of foo() nvm i thought about it more

One of the example is the Rcu::read_copy_update() function in this series. It guarantees only one updater can do the read-copy-update at a time, while allowing Rcu::deference() happens at the same time.

@Miguel Ojeda How much of the selection between polyfil and Upstream UnsafePinnedshould I include for V2?

From my side basically everything from only Polyfill to Config flag to select the Upsteam version seems reasonable (Although probably a middle path is best).

I am not sure I understand -- what do you mean by "how much"? If someone wants to use it, then there are only two possibilities, no? i.e. whether or not to use the upstream one in newer compiler versions.

Between those two, I would say let's keep it as simple as possible unless there is a good reason to use the upstream one.

In any case, we cannot use it yet, not even in 1.88, unless more PRs land quickly for 1.88, right?

Adding an UnsafePinned that we can replace with the upstream version eventually seems reasonable to me. I think we can probably wait with adding the config flag to select which one until the upstream one is more ready.

Also, sorry for not leaving a review on your miscdevice change. I looked at it previously and it looks quite reasonable, though it needs to be rebased because parts of miscdevice were reorganized in the meantime.

Miguel Ojeda said:

I am not sure I understand -- what do you mean by "how much"? If someone wants to use it, then there are only two possibilities, no? i.e. whether or not to use the upstream one in newer compiler versions.

Between those two, I would say let's keep it as simple as possible unless there is a good reason to use the upstream one.

I'll just go with that then.

In any case, we cannot use it yet, not even in 1.88, unless more PRs land quickly for 1.88, right?

Currently the upstream version should almost the exact same thing as my version from what I understand.

Alice Ryhl said:

Adding an UnsafePinned that we can replace with the upstream version eventually seems reasonable to me. I think we can probably wait with adding the config flag to select which one until the upstream one is more ready.

Also, sorry for not leaving a review on your miscdevice change. I looked at it previously and it looks quite reasonable, though it needs to be rebased because parts of miscdevice were reorganized in the meantime.

That's fine. I'm planning on sending UnsafePinned V2 after the next pin-init sync and then rebasing the miscdev changes on on the UnsafePinned patches.
